pdg_node.statement: (params: dict, reg: Register, opt_reg: Register, list_perm: list[int], weights_rev: np.ndarray[float]) -> dict[str, int]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Branch on register size: for < 22 atoms run the statevector simulator; for ≥ 22 atoms run the MPS emulator. Returns the raw measurement count distribution from simple_quantum_loop.

pdg_node.statement: (count_dist: dict[str, int], reg: Register, num_sol: int) -> tuple[list[set | str], dict[str, int]]

pdg_node.statement: (register_coord: dict/array of (x, y) tuples indexed by node id, graph: networkx.Graph) -> list of float
pdg_node.informal_desc: Iterates over all edges in the problem graph, retrieves the (x, y) coordinates of each endpoint from the register, and computes the Euclidean distance for every connected atom pair.

declaration.name: compute_connected_edge_distances
declaration.type_signature: (register_coord: dict/array of (x, y) tuples indexed by node id, graph: networkx.Graph) -> list of float
declaration.docstring: Iterates over all edges in the problem graph, retrieves the (x, y) coordinates of each endpoint from the register, and computes the Euclidean distance for every connected atom pair.

declaration.type_signature: (register_coord: dict/array of (x, y) tuples indexed by node id, graph: networkx.Graph) -> list of float
declaration.docstring: Iterates over all edges in the problem graph, retrieves the (x, y) coordinates of each endpoint from the register, and computes the Euclidean distance for every connected atom pair.

pdg_node.statement: (distance_list_connected: list of float, interaction_coefficient: float) -> float
pdg_node.informal_desc: Derives the minimum interaction energy U_min by applying the van-der-Waals interaction law U = C / r^6 to the maximum pairwise distance among connected edges, using the device interaction coefficient.

declaration.name: compute_u_min_from_connected_distances
declaration.type_signature: (distance_list_connected: list of float, interaction_coefficient: float) -> float
declaration.docstring: Derives the minimum interaction energy U_min by applying the van-der-Waals interaction law U = C / r^6 to the maximum pairwise distance among connected edges, using the device interaction coefficient.

declaration.name: compute_u_min_from_connected_distances
declaration.type_signature: (distance_list_connected: list of float, interaction_coefficient: float) -> float

declaration.docstring: Derives the minimum interaction energy U_min by applying the van-der-Waals interaction law U = C / r^6 to the maximum pairwise distance among connected edges, using the device interaction coefficient.

pdg_node.statement: (register_coord: dict/array of (x, y) tuples indexed by node id, graph: networkx.Graph) -> list of float
pdg_node.informal_desc: Constructs the complement of the problem graph (edges between non-connected atoms), retrieves the (x, y) coordinates for each complementary edge's endpoints, and computes the Euclidean distance for every disconnected atom pair.

declaration.name: compute_complementary_edge_distances
declaration.type_signature: (register_coord: dict/array of (x, y) tuples indexed by node id, graph: networkx.Graph) -> list of float
declaration.docstring: Constructs the complement of the problem graph (edges between non-connected atoms), retrieves the (x, y) coordinates for each complementary edge's endpoints, and computes the Euclidean distance for every disconnected atom pair.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Builds the interpolated amplitude and detuning waveforms for the global Rydberg channel using the fixed duration, omega breakpoints, and delta breakpoints that define the shaped adiabatic ramp.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Packages the amplitude and detuning waveforms together with zero phase into a single Pulse object representing one complete adiabatic drive on the global Rydberg channel.

pdg_node.statement: (duration: int, parameters: dict) -> ConstantWaveform
pdg_node.informal_desc: Creates a constant-valued DMM detuning waveform of the required duration, encoding the uniform local detuning offset applied to all atoms weighted by the detuning map.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Initialises a Pulser Sequence on the mock device, declares and configures the global Rydberg and DMM channels, then appends the Rydberg pulse and DMM detuning waveform to produce the complete, immutable adiabatic pulse schedule.

pdg_node.statement: (counts_mps_raw: dict[str, int], list_perm: list[int]) -> dict[str, int]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Applies the inverse of list_perm to every bitstring key returned by the MPS backend so that qubit indices correspond to the canonical problem ordering.

pdg_node.statement: (count_dist: dict[bitstring, int] — raw measurement counts from the sampler, register: Pulser Register — used for node-index to qubit-index mapping, num_solutions: int — number of top solutions to extract) -> tuple[list of node-set dicts ranked by measurement frequency, list of int — occurrence counts corresponding to each solution]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Post-processes the raw measurement count distribution to decode, rank, and filter the top-k bitstring solutions corresponding to valid independent sets (or QUBO ground states), mapping them back to the original graph node labelling.

declaration.name: quantum_solution_extractor
declaration.type_signature: (count_dist: dict[bitstring, int] — raw measurement counts from the sampler, register: Pulser Register — used for node-index to qubit-index mapping, num_solutions: int — number of top solutions to extract) -> tuple[list of node-set dicts ranked by measurement frequency, list of int — occurrence counts corresponding to each solution]
declaration.docstring: Post-processes the raw measurement count distribution to decode, rank, and filter the top-k bitstring solutions corresponding to valid independent sets (or QUBO ground states), mapping them back to the original graph node labelling.
declaration.conceptual_summary: FrequencyRankedTopKDecoder. Transforms a raw empirical frequency distribution over a discrete combinatorial symbol space (bitstrings) into a ranked, filtered, and semantically re-labeled subset of the k most probable feasible configurations. The operation applies a validity predicate to prune infeasible configurations, sorts survivors by descending observation frequency, and then applies an index-relabeling bijection to map internal symbolic indices back to the original external identifier namespace. The result is a lossy compression of the full distribution that retains only the highest-frequency, constraint-satisfying points, annotated with their original structural context.. Applications: Genomics / variant calling: given a multiset of observed read alignment patterns (bitstrings encoding SNP presence/absence across loci), extract the top-k most frequently observed haplotype configurations that satisfy biological feasibility constraints (e.g., Hardy-Weinberg consistency), remapping internal read indices back to chromosome position labels, Combinatorial optimization / scheduling: after stochastic local search or simulated annealing produces a histogram of visited assignment vectors, decode and rank the top-k feasible schedules (satisfying resource or precedence constraints) and re-label internal slot indices back to human-readable job/machine identifiers, Natural language processing / beam decoding: given a count distribution over discrete token-sequence samples from a language model, filter sequences satisfying a structural grammar predicate, rank by empirical frequency, and remap internal vocabulary integer IDs back to surface-form string tokens for the top-k outputs, Sensor fusion / fault detection: given a distribution of binary system-state snapshots (each bit encoding a sensor's alarm status) collected over a monitoring window, extract the k most frequently occurring fault-pattern configurations that match known failure-mode signatures, remapping bit positions back to physical sensor identifiers in the engineering schematic
